candidate_information.name: Jordan Michael Reyes
candidate_information.current_venturing_position_of_responsibi: Crew President, Venturing Officers Association (VOA) Vice President of Program – Three Rivers Council

leadership_and_positions.venturing_positions_recommendation_is_ba: Crew 728 President (2023–present); Crew 728 Vice President of Program (2021–2023); Three Rivers Council Venturing Officers Association (VOA) Vice President of Program (2022–present); Crew Guide and Instructor for new Venturers; Event chair for annual council-wide Venturing Fall Adventure Weekend.
leadership_and_positions.list_leadership_positions_held_related_t:
At the council level, Jordan has held several leadership positions that directly impact Venturing youth across the entire Three Rivers Council, not just within Crew 728.

1. Three Rivers Council Venturing Officers Association (VOA) Vice President of Program (2022–present)
As the VOA Vice President of Program, Jordan is responsible for planning, coordinating, and evaluating all council-level Venturing activities. Over the last two program years, Jordan has:
- Led the program team that organized three council-wide Venturing events each year: a Winter Lock-In, a Spring Service and Skills Weekend, and the Fall Adventure Weekend.
- Personally coordinated with four different council districts to rotate event locations so smaller or more rural crews would not always have to travel long distances, helping to increase attendance from an average of 45 youth at events in 2021 to over 80 youth at the 2024 Fall Adventure Weekend.
- Developed a standardized event planning checklist and after-action review form that is now used by the VOA and crew youth chairs for every council Venturing event. This process has led to more consistent communication, better budgeting, and improved safety planning. For example, after receiving feedback about cold-weather readiness at the 2023 Winter Lock-In, Jordan added a specific section on emergency gear and sleeping arrangements, which the council camping committee later adopted as a best practice.
- Worked closely with the council professional staff and the Council Venturing Committee Chair to coordinate promotion of events through email lists, social media, and roundtables. Jordan wrote sample announcements and social media posts that were shared with unit advisors and district executives so that every crew in the council received consistent invitations.

These responsibilities clearly operate at the council level, as Jordan’s work directly influences the quality and accessibility of the Venturing program for every crew in the Three Rivers Council. Rather than focusing solely on their own unit, Jordan has consistently made decisions based on what will benefit the greatest number of Venturers across the council.

2. Event Chair – Three Rivers Council Venturing Fall Adventure Weekend (2022, 2023, 2024)
Jordan has served as the youth event chair for the council’s Venturing Fall Adventure Weekend for three consecutive years. In this role, Jordan:
- Recruited and led a planning team of youth from six different crews to design the weekend program, which has included COPE (Challenging Outdoor Personal Experience) elements, climbing, kayaking, Leave No Trace workshops, and leadership development games.
- Coordinated with the council’s COPE and climbing committee to ensure that qualified instructors, safety officers, and equipment were available and that all activities met BSA safety and Guide to Safe Scouting standards.
- Implemented a “new Venturer track” with introductory sessions on basic camping skills, teamwork, and goal-setting, after noticing that many participants in 2022 were first-year Venturers who felt overwhelmed by the more advanced activities. Feedback from advisors indicated that this track significantly improved retention and confidence among new members.
- Managed the event budget in consultation with the council staff, including setting participant fees, tracking expenses, and preparing a financial report after each event. Jordan helped keep the event affordable by negotiating with a local outfitter for discounted equipment rental and working with the council development office to secure a small sponsorship from a local outdoor store.

Serving as event chair at this scale is inherently a council-level responsibility because it requires coordinating resources and participation from multiple districts and units, working closely with council committees, and ensuring that the event reflects the council’s program goals for Venturing.

3. Crew 728 President (2023–present)
As Crew President, Jordan’s leadership extends beyond the unit because Crew 728 has informally become a model crew within the council. In this role, Jordan has:
- Implemented a structured annual planning conference that includes goal-setting, budgeting, and a written program calendar, which has been shared with other crews through VOA workshops. Two newer crews in the council have adopted this planning method after attending Jordan’s presentations.
- Encouraged crew members to attend council and area Venturing events, resulting in Crew 728 consistently having one of the highest participation rates at VOA activities. Jordan makes a point of asking every crew member what types of events they want to see at the council level and then brings that feedback to VOA meetings.
- Mentored two younger Venturers who now serve as the crew’s Vice President of Administration and Vice President of Program, teaching them how to run effective meetings, communicate with advisors, and delegate tasks. This has strengthened the crew’s leadership pipeline and provided the council with youth who are prepared to take on VOA roles in the future.

While Crew President is technically a unit-level position, Jordan has used it to build a strong, visible, and well-organized crew that contributes actively to council Venturing activities. The way Jordan has approached this role—sharing best practices, encouraging participation, and developing new leaders—has had a clear impact beyond the crew and into the broader council Venturing program.

4. Trainer and Presenter at Council Venturing Roundtables and Training Events
Jordan frequently volunteers as a youth trainer at council roundtables and Venturing-specific training days. Examples include:
- Co-presenting a session on "Youth-Led Planning in Venturing" at the 2023 and 2024 council program kickoff, where Jordan walked adult advisors and youth officers through a sample planning meeting, using real Crew 728 materials as examples.
- Leading a workshop on "Making Venturing Welcoming and Inclusive" for new advisors, drawing on personal experience welcoming youth from different cultural and religious backgrounds into Crew 728. Jordan shared practical ideas, such as rotating meeting locations to be more accessible by public transportation and creating a crew culture statement that emphasizes respect and inclusion.
- Assisting with ILSC (Introduction to Leadership Skills for Crews) courses at the council level, facilitating small-group discussions on communication, conflict resolution, and servant leadership.

These training roles are directly related to the council-level scope of this award because they support leadership development across multiple units and help advisors and youth officers deliver a stronger Venturing program throughout the Three Rivers Council.

In summary, the positions and responsibilities outlined above show that Jordan’s impact is clearly at the council level. Through VOA leadership, council event planning, crew presidency with council-wide influence, and youth training roles, Jordan has consistently strengthened the overall Venturing program in the Three Rivers Council, making them a strong and appropriate candidate for the Council-level Venturing Leadership Award.
